items.claim: Yanz has good striking combinations, high volume, effective body work, and quick hands.
items.quote: Ya, solid strike with good combinations, good volume, he works the body well, and he's got quick hands.

items.claim: Yanz has demonstrated effective takedown defense in his previous fights.
items.quote: He's got good takedown defense as well. So, I don't think that the option of shooting takedowns for Garbrandt, which he can do, is going to be of uh the most success, right? As Yanz even showed that against Simone, he did a pretty good job stopping takedowns for the most part.

items.claim: Cody Garbrandt utilizes non-traditional, unpredictable striking combinations.
items.quote: The thing I like about Garbrandt is his combinations are much less traditional than you see for most people.

items.claim: Cody Garbrandt has diminished physical capabilities compared to his past performance.
items.quote: I don't think he has enough left in the tank here.

items.claim: Zach Reese struggles with range control despite his tall and lanky frame.
items.quote: The problem is he doesn't have very good range control. The dude's super tall and lanky, but he cannot seem to keep people at range.

items.claim: Zach Reese is overly willing to work from his back during grappling exchanges.
items.quote: he's willing to work from his back way too much. He's super comfortable from there.

items.claim: Ryan Gandra is effective at pressuring opponents and landing power shots when he traps them against the cage.
items.quote: he comes forward, he sends a barrage at you, and especially once he gets you up to the cage, he's willing to eat one to give one. And the guy it's he's hits pretty hard.
